title: Warm Buttery Moroccan Chicken Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful Moroccan dish featuring chicken cooked in a buttery and velvety sauce, perfect for a crowd, this recipe is kosher-friendly and incorporates shoyu-koji for added depth of flavor, resulting in an al-dente finish.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the chicken thighs and cook until they are browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the shoyu-koji, dried apricots, chicken broth, and unsalted butter to the pot, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened, about 25-30 minutes.
Season the tagine to taste with salt and pepper.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
